features.summary: Johnson & Johnson reported Q2 2022 results with worldwide sales of $24 billion, up 3% reported and 8% operational growth. Adjusted EPS was $2.59, up 4.4% reported and 10.9% operational. Pharmaceuticals grew 12.3% operationally driven by DARZALEX (+46%), STELARA (+19%), and ERLEADA (+57%). MedTech grew 3.4% operationally despite COVID mobility restrictions in Asia Pacific. Consumer Health grew 2.3% operationally, recovering from supply constraints. The company maintained full-year guidance of 7% operational sales growth and $10.70 adjusted EPS while tightening the EPS range.
features.management_outlook: Management remains confident in achieving long-term pharmaceutical growth targets of $60 billion by 2025 with above-market growth continuing through STELARA patent expiration. They expect MedTech momentum to continue with 6% growth in H1 2022 and market share gains in nearly all 11 priority platforms. The Consumer Health separation remains on track for 2023 completion with leadership team announced. Second half performance is expected to improve over first half across all segments, with Q4 slightly stronger than Q3.
features.risks: Currency headwinds are significant with $4 billion unfavorable impact on reported sales and $0.65 on EPS for full year, with $0.30-0.35 carrying into 2023. Inflationary pressures on labor, energy, and transportation costs are expected to persist through 2023, impacting margins. Regional COVID-19 mobility restrictions continue affecting Asia Pacific operations. Proposed Medicare drug pricing legislation could significantly impact R&D investment and innovation capabilities across the pharmaceutical industry.
features.key_drivers: DARZALEX subcutaneous conversion reached 85% in US and 80% in Europe, while new CAR-T therapy CARVYKTI launched successfully. TREMFYA gained market share in both psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis with strong 30% growth. MedTech benefited from 10 new product launches in H1 with improved commercial execution driving market share gains. Consumer Health recovery was supported by strategic price increases and improving supply constraints, particularly in Skin Health/Beauty franchise.
